intent-over-implementation.notes: FAIL. (1) 'processAccountClosure' uses the vague verb 'process' which is implementation-oriented. Should convey intent: e.g. closeAccount, finalizeAccountClosure. (2) Parameter named 'data' is completely generic -- should be 'account' or 'accountData' at minimum to convey intent. (3) 'formatter' parameter is declared but NEVER USED in the function body -- dead parameter.

symmetry.notes: startTimestamp/endTimestamp show proper symmetry. closureResult/amount/user return shape is consistent.

no-mental-mapping.notes: FAIL. 'data' requires mental mapping -- reader must look at the type annotation (AccountData) to understand what it represents. The parameter name alone communicates nothing.

explicit-units.notes: elapsedMs has explicit millisecond unit suffix. startTimestamp/endTimestamp imply time. bufferSize could be more explicit (bufferSizeBytes?) but is borderline acceptable.

no-acronyms.notes: No problematic acronyms. 'Ms' is a standard abbreviation for milliseconds.

critical_issues: formatter parameter is declared but never used -- dead code that should have been removed or utilized.
Function name 'processAccountClosure' violates intent-over-implementation with the vague verb 'process'.
Parameter 'data: AccountData' violates both intent-over-implementation and no-mental-mapping.
